title: salix fluviatilis f. villosa
intro: Salix fluviatilis f. villosa, also known as willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Salicaceae family, characterized by its grayish-brown bark, slender branches, and lance-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Salix fluviatilis f. villosa lie in its salicylic acid content, which is similar to aspirin, making it a natural anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of this plant include relief from pain, reducing inflammation, and treating fever, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. The herbal preparations of Salix fluviatilis f. villosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other illnesses.
